            cell [e163] [cursor=pointer]
              generic "Complete: ```json { \"content\": \"Within SPARTA technique EX-0005.01 (Design Flaws), controls CM0024 (Anti-counterfeit Hardware) and CM0028 (Tamper Protection) are complementary security measures that address different aspects of hardware integrity protection against design flaw exploitation.\n\nCM0024 focuses on preventing counterfeit components from entering the system through policy and procedures, including tamper resistance and protection measures. According to the evidence, CM0024 \\"develops and implements anti-counterfeit policy and procedures designed to detect and prevent counterfeit components from entering the information system, including tamper resistance and protection.\\" This control addresses the supply chain aspect of hardware security, ensuring components are authentic before they are integrated into spacecraft systems.\n\nCM0028 focuses on physical inspection and tamper-proof protection mechanisms. The evidence states that CM0028 involves \\"perform[ing] physical inspection of hardware to look for potential tampering\\" and \\"leverag[ing] tamper proof protection where possible when shipping/receiving equipment.\\" This control addresses the detection of physical tampering through inspection and the implementation of anti-tamper mechanisms.\n\nThe relationship between these controls in the context of EX-0005.01 is that they provide layered protection against hardware design flaw exploitation. CM0024 prevents counterfeit components (which may contain intentional design flaws or vulnerabilities) from entering the system, while CM0028 detects and prevents physical tampering that could exploit existing design flaws. Both controls contribute to ensuring hardware integrity, which is critical for mitigating threats against hardware design flaws as described in EX-0005.01.\", \"citations\": [\"EX-0005.01\", \"CM0024\", \"CM0028\"], \"confidence\": \"high\", \"caveats\": [\"The evidence case does not contain explicit statements about the direct relationship between CM0024 and CM0028, only their individual relationships to EX-0005.01. The synthesized relationship is inferred from their complementary functions as described in the glossary definitions and QRA evidence.\"] } ```" [e164] [cursor=pointer]
